events.title: The Science of Chocolate Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. People have been obsessed with chocolate for over 5,000 years. The Olmec and Mayan people used cocoa beans to make chocolatey drinks. The Aztecs used cocoa beans as currency and to make tasty treats. However, the first chocolate bar didn't show up until 1847 and has only gotten better since. This camp is great for students interested in fermentation, roasting, and finding out how we can change the texture and flavor of this amazing treat. Join us to discover the incredible science behind chocolate production. Science never tasted so good! The camp fee is $20. Register: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: ServSafe® Training
events.description: The purpose of the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program is to help food service operations protect public health by preventing foodborne illness and promoting safe food-handling practices. In response to increased publicity surrounding foodborne illness outbreaks and growing consumer concern, this program provides nationally recognized, science-based training developed by the National Restaurant Association. Through ServSafe(R) Manager and ServSafe(R) Food Handler training, participants gain the knowledge and skills needed to provide safe food, reduce contamination risks, and maintain clean, sanitary facilities. The program prepares managers and employees to implement effective food safety management systems, follow proper purchasing, storage, preparation, service procedures, cleaning and sanitation, and train staff in best practices. Intended Audience: The ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program, developed by the National Restaurant Association, addresses growing food safety concerns by providing science-based training for food service professionals. ServSafe(R) Manager is designed for food service managers and supervisors in restaurants, hospitals, schools, long-term care facilities, and other food-handling operations. It offers nationally recognized certification and covers current food safety principles and best practices. ServSafe(R) Food Handler is a basic food safety course for food service employees. It covers essential topics including personal hygiene, cross-contamination and allergens, time and temperature control, and cleaning and sanitation. Certification: By successfully completing the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program training, food service professionals can earn National Restaurant Association certification, update their food safety skills using the latest FDA Food Code guidance, and strengthen their operation's commitment to food safety and customer confidence.In-PersonStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Staff Senate District 7: Research District-Wide Meeting
events.description: Staff members are invited to attend the upcoming District 7 Staff Senate district-wide meeting! This is a vital opportunity to connect directly with your Staff Senators, receive updates on current campus initiatives, and contribute your voice to important discussions that affect the UNL staff community. District 7 Open Houses from 10-11am quarterly on third Wednesdays in 2026 January 21: East Campus Prairie A April 15: City Campus Union Chimney Rock Room July 15: Innovation Campus Board Room October 21: Whittier 152 Virtual access: https://unl.zoom.us/j/93631255766 Your Voice Matters! Join us to hear the latest from the Senate, ask questions, share your feedback, concerns, and ideas, and network with colleagues. Let’s work together to ensure effective staff representation and make UNL an even better workplace.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: NRICH: Husker Hospitality
events.description: Learn the strategies for delivering excellent service by emphasizing understanding customer needs and exceeding their expectations. Learn the importance of recognizing all interaction points and proactively anticipating customer needs. You'll receive practical advice on effective communication, including active listening, using clear language, and offering helpful alternatives. Furthermore, you'll learn a process for resolving conflicts by emphasizing empathy, listening, action, and restoring customer satisfaction. You'll come away with actionable steps to create positive and memorable service experiences. Presented by Ranelle Maltas, Training, Technology Specialist Biography: Ranelle Maltas has been a dedicated member of the University of Nebraska-Lincoln (UNL) community since 2000, when she began her journey as an Application Trainer for Business & Finance. From 2007 to 2018, she served as the Learning & Development Manager for ITS, spearheading initiatives to enhance technology learning. In September 2018, Ranelle transitioned to the Office of Human Resources, where she took on a multifaceted role developing and presenting training programs, coordinating the New Employee Orientation, and overseeing the HR website. Currently, as a key contributor to HR's Organizational Development & Training team, Ranelle focuses on designing and coordinating professional development opportunities to empower staff and foster growth across UNL. Beyond her professional work, Ranelle is an active member of the community. She serves on the Board of Directors of the UNL Band Alumni Association and is the Vice President of Technology for the National Association of Alumni Bands. Additionally, she shares her expertise by teaching Excel courses for the SCC Continuing Education Center. On a personal note, Ranelle is a proud mother of a UNL graduate, a devoted wife to Bill, and co-owner of the Waffleman(TM). . Click the link below to register:Status: CONFIRMED | -

events.description: Whether you are a homeowner, gardener, or you love nature's wondrous pageantry, Backyard Farmer is your reliable resource for Expert Advice in horticulture, plant pathology, entomology, wildlife, and turf management. Every week throughout the growing season, we are here to answer all of your gardening and landscape questions. events.title: Clifton Strengths Institute Summer Conference
events.description: Designed for higher education leaders and champions of strengths-based programming in their community, this conference includes a variety of thought-provoking sessions designed to highlight excellence in strengths-based education on campuses and in the workplace. Networking, motivating keynote speakers and interactive discussions are a few highlights of the Clifton Strengths Institute Summer Conference. Breakout sessions will offer opportunities to network and collaborate with fellow strengths enthusiasts. Registration includes lunch. Attendees are not required to complete the CliftonStrengths Assessment prior to attending the conference, but completing it beforehand can enrich your experience. Registration is $59, early bird registration through May 1 saves people 15%. Learn more and register at: https://go.unl.edu/csisummerconference. Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Snack Attack: Apple Edition Camp
events.description: Young Nebraska Scientists (YNS) conducted by Nebraska EPSCoR with funding from the U.S. National Science Foundation (NSF) - offers camps for middle and high school students throughout the state. YNS experiences focus on Science, Technology, Engineering and Math (STEM) topics, and are a great way for teens to sample a career, meet friends and mentors, and find their future! NSF fundings helps keep YNS camp fees low; and need-based scholarships are readily available. Faculty from Nebraska colleges and universities are camp leaders, and camp staff include university students. This is a hands-on, two-day food science camp where students explore how simple, recognizable ingredients can be intentionally processed into smart, functional snacks. Working in a clean, design-focused lab environment, students deconstruct apples to understand their structure, transform them using different processing methods, and reflect on how food design choices affect health and sustainability. This camp is ideal for students curious about food science, clean-label products, sustainability, and how everyday snacks are engineered with purpose. This camp is sponsored by the Kimmel Foundation. Camp fee is $40. Apply: https://yns.nebraska.edu/gain-hands-on-experience-in-stem/summer-camps. Early application deadline is May 1. Priority is given to high-school students. After May 1, middle-school students may apply, if space is available.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Nebraska Safety Center Driver Education
events.description: Two driver education classes will be offered this summer right here at Nebraska Innovation Campus in the Rise building! Driver Education is one of the most important courses a student will take because it provides them with the foundation on which to build their knowledge and skills for safe driving for the rest of their lives. As a result, our classes are small (limited to no more than 24 students), and focus on the basics of driving skills, safety, attitudes and behaviors. The course consists of 20 or more hours of classroom instruction and 5 hours of behind-the-wheel driving instruction. Passing the course results in the waiver of written and driving tests at the Nebraska DMV unless the student is 18 or older. Persons 18 or older will receive a certificate but are required to test at the DMV. The cost of the course is $380. The registration deadline is two weeks prior to the start of the course. Register: https://www.unk.edu/offices/safety_center/driver-education.php.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Flyover Fintech
events.description: Today, U.S. Congressman Mike Flood announced that he will host his next Flyover Fintech conference on August 4, 2026, at Nebraska Innovation Campus in Lincoln. "Flyover Fintech has become an annual tradition where public and private sector leaders from across the country gather in Lincoln at Nebraska Innovation Campus to talk about the future of fintech," said Congressman Flood. "Whether it's digital assets, housing, AI, and more, Flyover Fintech has something to offer for everyone. Space is limited, so I encourage you to check out the website and get registered today." Flyover Fintech conferences feature a wide variety of speakers, ranging from members of the House Financial Services Committee to leading voices in the fintech industry. Speakers and panelists for Flyover Fintech 2026 will be announced in the coming months. Registration for the conference is now open at www.flyoverfintech.com. Questions about the conference can be directed to Jake Dumas at jake.dumas@mail.house.gov.A conference bringing innovators, financial services leaders and policymakers to the center of everything fintech. Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: 26th Annual Nebraska Grazing Conference
events.description: Make plans now to attend the 2026 Nebraska Grazing Conference at the Younes Conference Center in Kearney, NE on August 4-5. The theme of the conference will be Celebrating Grazing on Nebraska's Grasslands as part the 2026 International Year of Rangelands and Pastoralists. The conference will begin on afternoon of Tuesday, August 4. Tradeshow talks on the 4th will focus on Virtual Fence Technologies for better grazing management followed by a celebration of the 2026 International Year of Rangelands and Pastoralists. On Wednesday, August 5, a full day of speakers will be focused on regenerative grazing management, grazing on croplands, small ruminant grazing, conservation grazing practices, and grazing stockmanship. Keynote speaker's include Gail Fuller (https://farmersfootprint.us/gail-fuller) who will speak on regenerative grazing management practices. Dr. Tom Noffsinger will highlight the importance of stockmanship in grazing management. Hother speakers will present on Carbon Credit markets and opportunities on grazing lands. High school and university students are encouraged to attend. Student registration fees are waived for those registering online before July 15. https://grassland.unl.edu/nebraska-grazing-conference-registration/ Celebrating Grazing on Nebraska's GrasslandsStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: From Idea to Startup Workshop
events.description: This two-day workshop will provide tools for aspiring entrepreneurs who want to start their own business. It will cover topics including idea validation, entity formation and funding a startup. Local business founders will discuss their entrepreneurial journeys, and guest speakers will share resources about intellectual property protection, business accelerator programs, grants and other support programs. There also will be opportunities to network with other campus innovators and experts from the Nebraska startup ecosystem. Audience: This workshop is open to all university of Nebraska faculty, staff, postdocs, students, and community partners as well. The workshop will be especially helpful for startups based on university intellectual property (IP). However, you do not need to have a current startup idea or IP to attend. Questions: Joy Eakin, jeakin@nutechventures.org, entrepreneurship program manager, NUtech Ventures. Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.description: This 1-day (8-hour) course prepares students to pass the FAA Part 107 Aeronautical Knowledge Exam required to receive a remote pilot certificate (aka drone license). Covers aviation topics such as regulations, airspace, radio communication, weather, aircraft performance, and other basic aviation concepts necessary to pass the exam. Aviation experience is not essential to be successful in the course. This course is geared towards anyone interested in flying drones for commercial or public applications, including agriculture, spray drones, emergency management, law enforcement, real estate and land management, and photography. events.title: Native American Film Series
events.location: Mary Riepma Ross Media Arts Center-Van Brunt Visitors Center Room:Ross Theater (123)
events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.description: You will receive valuable insights and resources during your orientation. • Get acquainted with the vibrant community and inclusive culture at the university. • Connect with fellow new employees from various departments across campus and engage in meaningful conversations. • Dive into our strategic plan and explore how it aligns with your role and goals within the university. • Gain valuable insights from a senior leader who will share their experiences and vision for the university’s future. This session will also provide an opportunity for you to ask questions. • Learn about the comprehensive benefits package available to you as an employee of the university. Discover how to make the most of your benefits options and access the resources designed to support your well-being and professional development.for all new hires, rehires, campus-transfers, and those moving from temp to regular statusStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Poster Sale
events.description: Browse, enjoy, and purchase from hundreds and hundreds of different prints to decorate your residence hall room, apartment, chapter house, or office. Choose from a wide selection of smash-hit classic posters of famous art and images to fresh-off-the-press artist prints, and so much more. Studies have shown that a vibrant living space and the use of sensory images can positively impact learning and well-being. This annual Poster Sale provides an easy on-campus opportunity to purchase art and other media to liven up your living space and express your individual self in a creative and easy way. Purchases can be made with credit credits and Apple pay. This five-day sale is 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily August 31 - September 4, 2026 in the Nebraska Union's Memorial Plaza. In case of rain or inclement weather, the sale will relocate indoors to Nebraska Union's The Crib.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Peace Corps Prep Application Deadline
events.description: Integrating UNL coursework, hands-on experience, and professional development, Nebraska’s Peace Corps Prep program prepares undergraduates for volunteer service globally, potentially with the Peace Corps, by building the following four core competencies: 1) Sector-specific skills (in Education, Health, Environment, Agriculture, Youth in Development, or Community Economic Development) 2) Foreign language proficiency 3) Intercultural competence 4) Professional savvy and leadership Upon completion of the program, you will receive a certificate from the Peace Corps, a federal agency. Completing the PC Prep program gives you a competitive edge when applying for Peace Corps service, but does not guarantee acceptance as a Peace Corps Volunteer. Special features of the PC Prep program at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln include: *Eligibility for competitive scholarship to study or intern abroad in a country where Peace Corps volunteers have served. *Tailored education abroad advising and Intercultural Development Inventory (IDI) assessment upon acceptance and completion. *Social/networking events, group volunteer experiences, and announcements about related campus and community events. *Opportunities to engage with Returned Peace Corps Volunteers in the community through events and mentorship. *Culminating zero-credit course listed on the transcript, recognition in campus media outlets, and a cord to wear at graduation. Learn more and start your application today!FallStatus: CONFIRMED |

events.description: Whether you are a homeowner, gardener, or you love nature's wondrous pageantry, Backyard Farmer is your reliable resource for Expert Advice in horticulture, plant pathology, entomology, wildlife, and turf management. Every week throughout the growing season, we are here to answer all of your gardening and landscape questions. Our weekly advice is tailored to the gardening season, addressing weather challenges and tasks relevant at different times of the year. This helps gardeners know what to do and when to do it, from planting and pruning to lawn care and pest control. Our experts will help you diagnose problems and offer effective, environmentally friendly solutions. Backyard Farmer Television showStatus: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: Poster Sale
events.description: Browse, enjoy, and purchase from hundreds and hundreds of different prints to decorate your residence hall room, apartment, chapter house, or office. Choose from a wide selection of smash-hit classic posters of famous art and images to fresh-off-the-press artist prints, and so much more. Studies have shown that a vibrant living space and the use of sensory images can positively impact learning and well-being. This annual Poster Sale provides an easy on-campus opportunity to purchase art and other media to liven up your living space and express your individual self in a creative and easy way. Purchases can be made with credit credits and Apple pay. This five-day sale is 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily August 31 - September 4, 2026 in the Nebraska Union's Memorial Plaza. In case of rain or inclement weather, the sale will relocate indoors to Nebraska Union's The Crib.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.description: Now in it's third year, the Native American Film Series at The Ross features free monthly screenings showcasing Native American films and television programs from the Vision Maker Media public broadcasting archives. Presented by The Ross Media Arts Center, Vision Maker Media, and Friends of The Ross. Unless otherwise noted, screenings in this series will take place on the second Monday of each month and are admission free and open to the public. Tickets available at the Ross box office (online ticketing not available for free screenings). Showing in April: SCHA'NEXW ELHTAL'NEXW SALMON PEOPLE: PRESERVING A WAY OF LIFE is an hour-long documentary inspired by the late Chexanexwh Larry Kinley, a Lummi fisherman and tribal leader who embodied a belief in tribal sovereignty. The film follows two Lummi families fishing for sockeye. As they come to grips with a depleting fishery, Larry asks: "Who Are We Without Salmon?" Celebrating the resilience and adaptive natures of salmon and the people, the film is a reflection on a spiritual life way centered on respect and gratitude for salmon. Ross EventStatus: CONFIRMED | |

events.title: Health Equity Grand Rounds: Ciera Kirkpatrick
events.description: Ciera Kirkpatrick, PhD, will join us for our October Grand Rounds to discuss "Scrolling Through Motherhood: How Social Media Shapes Maternal Mental Health." Today's mothers are navigating motherhood in a world saturated with social media. From carefully curated parenting posts on Instagram to postpartum "bounce back" fitspiration videos on TikTok, social media is exacerbating the culture of comparison and shaping maternal mental health in ways we are only beginning to understand. In this talk, Dr. Kirkpatrick will discuss findings from a series of experiments examining how idealized social media content influences mothers' anxiety, shame, body image, and confidence in their parenting abilities. She will also discuss a promising strategy for reducing the harms of social media comparisons. This talk will bring attention to social media's effects on mental health and what we can do about it. Dr. Kirkpatrick is an Assistant Professor of Advertising and Public Relations at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln College of Journalism and Mass Communications. Her research uses a social scientific experimental approach to investigate how messaging in the media (e.g., advertising, news, social media content) affects individuals' mental and physical health and how strategic communicators can effectively design messages to improve individuals' health outcomes. Learn more about Dr. Kirkpatrick: https://journalism.unl.edu/person/ciera-kirkpatrick/ This event is FREE and open to the public. Register here for the Zoom link: https://unl.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_MefhgDCBSey4eFd_f4h6sg#/registration Minority Health Disparities Initiative Status: CONFIRMED | -

events.title: ServSafe® Training
events.description: The purpose of the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program is to help food service operations protect public health by preventing foodborne illness and promoting safe food-handling practices. In response to increased publicity surrounding foodborne illness outbreaks and growing consumer concern, this program provides nationally recognized, science-based training developed by the National Restaurant Association. Through ServSafe(R) Manager and ServSafe(R) Food Handler training, participants gain the knowledge and skills needed to provide safe food, reduce contamination risks, and maintain clean, sanitary facilities. The program prepares managers and employees to implement effective food safety management systems, follow proper purchasing, storage, preparation, service procedures, cleaning and sanitation, and train staff in best practices. Intended Audience: The ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program, developed by the National Restaurant Association, addresses growing food safety concerns by providing science-based training for food service professionals. ServSafe(R) Manager is designed for food service managers and supervisors in restaurants, hospitals, schools, long-term care facilities, and other food-handling operations. It offers nationally recognized certification and covers current food safety principles and best practices. ServSafe(R) Food Handler is a basic food safety course for food service employees. It covers essential topics including personal hygiene, cross-contamination and allergens, time and temperature control, and cleaning and sanitation. Certification: By successfully completing the ServSafe(R) Food Safety Program training, food service professionals can earn National Restaurant Association certification, update their food safety skills using the latest FDA Food Code guidance, and strengthen their operation's commitment to food safety and customer confidence.In-PersonStatus: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Nebraska Innovation Campus Blood Drive
events.description: American Red Cross will be at Nebraska Innovation Campus on Wednesday, October 21 from 10:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. NIC Partners and Lincoln community are encouraged to donate blood. Every day, blood donors help patients of all ages: accident and burn victims, heart surgery and organ transplant patients, and those battling cancer. In fact, every two seconds, someone in the U.S. needs blood. To make an appointment to donate blood, visit: https://www.redcrossblood.org/give.html/drive-results?zipSponsor=NebraskaInnovationCampus. The blood drive will take place in The Club, the private event space connected to The Mill Coffee & Bistro.Status: CONFIRMED | - |

events.title: Peace Corps Prep Application Deadline
events.description: Integrating UNL coursework, hands-on experience, and professional development, Nebraska’s Peace Corps Prep program prepares undergraduates for volunteer service globally, potentially with the Peace Corps, by building the following four core competencies: 1) Sector-specific skills (in Education, Health, Environment, Agriculture, Youth in Development, or Community Economic Development) 2) Foreign language proficiency 3) Intercultural competence 4) Professional savvy and leadership Upon completion of the program, you will receive a certificate from the Peace Corps, a federal agency. Completing the PC Prep program gives you a competitive edge when applying for Peace Corps service, but does not guarantee acceptance as a Peace Corps Volunteer. Special features of the PC Prep program at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln include: *Eligibility for competitive scholarship to study or intern abroad in a country where Peace Corps volunteers have served. *Tailored education abroad advising and Intercultural Development Inventory (IDI) assessment upon acceptance and completion. *Social/networking events, group volunteer experiences, and announcements about related campus and community events. *Opportunities to engage with Returned Peace Corps Volunteers in the community through events and mentorship. *Culminating zero-credit course listed on the transcript, recognition in campus media outlets, and a cord to wear at graduation. Learn more and start your application today!SpringStatus: CONFIRMED |

events.title: Nebraska Open tournament
events.description: This will be a two-day badminton tournament. We would like to have this event start at 8:30am and end 6:00pm for both days. This event is open to the public, we will be expecting people from around the country to attend. Last year we had around 100 participants. We will need 2-3 microphones, dry-erase boards and dry erase markers, 6-8 clipboards, 6-8 long tables, two racks of chairs (50 chairs), 2-3 speakers, aux cord, and scissors. We will also need 9 courts set up for this event. If it is possible, would we be able to come into the rec before 8:30amto set up for this event? If there are any questions or concerns please reach out.

events.title: Alpha Gamma Barnyard
events.description: Alpha Gamma Barnyard is a philanthropy event hosted by Alpha Gamma Sigma designed to raise funds for Multiple Sclerosis research and awareness. The event will take place on campus and will feature a family-friendly petting zoo experience that highlights the organization’s agricultural roots while engaging both the University of Nebraska–Lincoln community and the greater Lincoln area. Funds will be raised primarily through ticket sales for admission to the event. Tickets will be available both in advance and at the door on the day of the event. Advance tickets will be promoted and sold through members of Alpha Gamma Sigma and through online promotion on the organization’s social media platforms. On the day of the event, tickets will also be available for purchase at the entrance. Members of Alpha Gamma Sigma, under the direction of the Philanthropy Chair and the executive team, will be responsible for managing ticket sales, collecting payments, and monitoring the entrance area. All proceeds from ticket sales will be donated to support Multiple Sclerosis. Daryl Warday was a AGS Alumni who passed away from MS.

events.title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

History Club is a campus RSO designed for undergraduate history students and history lovers at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln. Club meetings consist of guest speakers, professional and academic development opportunities, and history trivia nights. Most of all, this club is designed to build a community of peers within the History Department and expand that community by introducing a network of professional and academic opportunities. events.title: College Republicans Weekly Meeting
events.description: Come hang out with the College Republicans! We are a registered group on campus dedicated to fostering and encouraging the activities of the Republican Party and promoting its ideals. We serve as an effective political voice through discussing and disseminating information on the local, state, national, and international levels. Our organization serves as a quasi-lobby in an effort to influence governmental affairs on a local, state, and national level. We encourage college students to express their political views through the philosophy of the Republican Party. Republicans believe in liberty, economic prosperity, preserving American values and traditions, and restoring the American dream for every citizen of this great nation. Through College Republicans, we wish to bring the next generation of Republicans into the party in order to grow and bring new ideas to the Republican Party through debate and discussion. We extend an invite to anyone on campus, regardless of their political orientation, background, or interest level to attend our weekly meetings to discuss the political issues going on in our local towns, cities, states, and nations, and have some fun!

Join CJSA for our fourth annual agency field day! Interact with a variety of criminal justice agencies (law enforcement, courts, corrections) to explore your interests and future internships and careers. Engage with agencies' interactive elements and enjoy some food! New this year: a focus on mental health and well-being while working in the criminal justice field. We will have a display of mental health resources, advice from criminal justice professionals, a buffet of wellness items, and therapy dogs! See you there!

events.title: “Something better even than the Bible!”: Flannery O’Connor’s Re-presentation of Salvation History
events.description: “Something better even than the Bible!”: Flannery O’Connor’s Re-presentation of Salvation History | A lecture by Prof. Alex Taylor (Christendom College) Join the Thomistic Institute at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln for an evening lecture given by Prof. Alex Taylor of Christendom College! The lecture will run from 6:15PM until 7:15PM with Q&A to follow. About the Speaker: Alex Taylor is an Assistant Professor of English Language and Literature at Christendom College. He was previously Cowan Fellow for Criticism at the University of Dallas in Irving, TX, where he wrote a dissertation that unearthed the shared political vision of two 20th century Catholic novelists, Flannery O'Connor and Evelyn Waugh. He has published reviews of fiction, nonfiction, and film as well as essays and poetry (translations and original lyrics) in various magazines, including the National Catholic Register, Word on Fire's Evangelization and Culture Online, Front Porch Republic, Presence: A Journal of Catholic Poetry, Dappled Things, Law & Liberty, The European Conservative and The American Conservative.

events.title: Earth Day
events.description: The Lutheran Center will be hosting a special Earth Day service on April 22 from 7:30-8:30 in partnership with Sustain UNL. This event is designed to bring together faith and environmental stewardship by creating space for reflection, education, and action. Representatives from Sustain UNL will share practical and accessible sustainability initiatives that students can incorporate into their daily lives. Their presentation will focus on promoting environmental responsibility and empowering individuals to make a positive impact within the campus and broader community. The event will also include a brief, reflective worship service centered on gratitude for creation and prayer for the care and restoration of the Earth. This service aims to foster a sense of shared responsibility and spiritual connection to environmental stewardship. All students are welcome to attend and participate in this meaningful opportunity to learn, reflect, and take action in celebration of Earth Day.

events.title: Phi Delt Masters
events.description: Our event is a 9-Hole Mini Golf Fundraiser hosted at the Phi Delta Theta fraternity house on campus. The purpose of this event is to raise funds and awareness for ALS. The fraternity house will be temporarily transformed into a creative, indoor mini golf course consisting of nine themed holes set up throughout common areas of the house. Each hole will be safely constructed using lightweight, temporary materials such as turf mats, PVC barriers, ramps, and obstacles. The course will be supervised at all times by designated fraternity members to ensure safety, organization, and flow of participants. Groups of 3–4 participants will rotate through the course in timed intervals to prevent overcrowding and maintain structure. Funds will be raised primarily through ticket sales. Tickets will be sold both in advance and at the door on the day of the event. Advance tickets will be promoted through social media, campus flyers, and word-of-mouth, and sold via Venmo or cash directly to designated event chairs. Day-of-event tickets will be available at a check-in table located at the entrance of the fraternity house. Ticket prices will be $10 per individual or $30 per team of four. All ticket sales will be tracked by the event treasurer and philanthropy chair. Two members will be stationed at the entrance at all times to manage payments, issue wristbands or scorecards, and monitor attendance. The event will also include a “Hole-in-One Challenge” with a small prize incentive. The event will be open to all UNL students and invited guests. Fraternity members will serve as course supervisors, crowd monitors, and clean-up crew to ensure the house is maintained and all safety guidelines are followed. The event will last approximately three hours.

events.title: Black Excellence Festival
events.description: This event is a community-centered celebration designed to support Black students’ academic success, professional development, wellness, and cultural connection. The Higher Education & Career Zone will feature panels and workshops led by Black faculty, professionals, and alumni, offering academic support, career readiness resources, mentorship, networking opportunities, and a recognition ceremony honoring Black student achievements. The Health & Wellness Village will provide free physical health screenings, fitness and movement sessions, mindfulness workshops, and health education booths staffed by Black health professionals. The Black Business & Community Marketplace will highlight local Black-owned businesses, student entrepreneurs, and community organizations, creating space for economic support, engagement, and pride. Main stage programming will include a keynote speaker, live music, spoken word and artistic performances, and additional recognition of academic excellence. The event is supported through partnerships with student organizations, university offices, community organizations, and local Black-owned businesses, with funding allocated toward speakers and performers, wellness resources, vendor support, food, marketing, giveaways, and necessary permits and logistics to ensure a welcoming and impactful experience.

events.title: Alumni Meet

The gathering will focus on networking, mentorship, and the exchange of ideas, creating opportunities for students to gain insights from alumni across various professional fields. Attendees can expect an interactive environment that encourages collaboration, guidance, and strengthening of the NSA community. This event aims to foster lasting connections while celebrating the shared experiences and achievements of NSA members.

events.title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

The sale will feature work made by School of Art, Art History and Design graduate and undergraduate print students. The hours will run Friday, May 1st from 9am-6pm and Saturday, May 2nd from 9am-5pm and will be located in Richards Hall room RH112 The event is free and open to the public. The Print Club Sale will include works of art created by graduate and undergraduate students in the School of Art, Art History and Design representing a broad range of makers, aesthetics, techniques and concepts. Proceeds from the sale go to the artists, as well as funding future Print Club events, visiting artists, and conference fees.

events.title: 67th Annual Cornhusker College Rodeo
events.description: Event Overview Join us in Lincoln, Nebraska, for one of the longest-running traditions in collegiate rodeo—the 67th Annual Cornhusker College Rodeo! Hosted by the University of Nebraska Rodeo Association (UNRA) and sanctioned by the National Intercollegiate Rodeo Association (NIRA), this two-day event showcases the top student-athletes from across the Great Plains Region. Over 340 contestants from six states — Nebraska, South Dakota, North Dakota, Iowa, Wisconsin, and Minnesota — will compete in nine NIRA-sanctioned events at the Sandhills Global Event Center’s Tractor House Pavilion, “Amy Countryman Arena.”

events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

events.title: Nvolvement Fair on City Campus
events.description: In a single location, explore more than 100 recognized student organizations (RSOs) to join for social, professional and leadership interests. RSO members and officers will be on hand to provide details about their organization and answer questions from prospective new members. This event is free and casual in nature. All Huskers are encouraged to get involved in at least two organizations during their college years: one associated with academics and one associated with an interest. Joining an organization is an easy way to meet new people, learn a new hobby or interest, or pursue something for which you are passionate. A second Involvement Fair is from 3 to 5 p.m. August 28 at the Nebraska East Union.

events.title: UPC Nebraska Fall 2026 Poster Sale with Poster Invasion
events.description: Browse, enjoy, and purchase from hundreds and hundreds of different prints to decorate your residence hall room, apartment, chapter house, or office. Choose from a wide selection of smash-hit classic posters of famous art and images to fresh-off-the-press artist prints, and so much more. Studies have shown that a vibrant living space and the use of sensory images can positively impact learning and well-being. This annual Poster Sale provides an easy on-campus opportunity to purchase art and other media to liven up your living space and express your individual self in a creative and easy way. Purchases can be made with credit credits and Apple pay. This five-day sale is 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily August 31s - September 4th in the Nebraska Union’s Arcarde Plaza, or in "The Crib" (rain site).

Crafts range from anything from crochet to balloon animals, so check out our Instagram (@mindandcraftunl) or GroupMe to learn where and what we're doing this week! We provide all materials for the planned craft of the night for free, but feel free to bring your own supplies if you have a project you're working on! Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/85160453/P2ewfbEV

events.title: Shotokan Ohshima Karate-do & Self-Defense
events.description: Please see our website and Google calendar for the most up-to-date practice schedule: https://unl.ska.org/karate-class-schedule/ Shotokan Ohshima is a very traditional style and differs from many martial arts schools. Comprehensive, challenging exercise is only one of the many benefits of karate practice. Teaching traditional karate-do as a serious art and way of life means much more. It ensures the original ideas of the founder are passed on to our students, so that we grow in all aspects of our lives. Altman Senpai pushes us to challenge ourselves and taste our life, to be more fully aware and conscientious at all times. Our karate practice can be very difficult. The main difficulty lies in training one's mind to be disciplined and focused. Remember, anyone can be lazy, because that is the easiest thing to do! Karate may be defined as a weapon-less means of self-defense. These techniques use all parts of the body to their maximum advantage. Our fundamental techniques are divided into three main aspects: - Kihon — Fundamental blocks, punches, kicks, and stances- Kata — Pre-arranged forms simulating combat situations- Kumite — Sparring In each category, the beginner is given instruction at the most basic level until the techniques become spontaneous to them. As the student progresses technically, the practice progresses physically and the student begins to build greater stamina. At this stage, one involves himself or herself with the more intricate and difficult kata and more dynamic forms of kumite. Karate as self-defense is one of the most dynamic of all the martial arts. The trained karate student is able to coordinate the mind and body perfectly, thereby allowing one to unleash tremendous physical power at will. It is not the possession of great physical strength that makes a strong karateka; rather it is the ability to coordinate the mind and body. Upon developing this ability, even the smallest person finds that within themselves is the power to deliver a devastating blow to any would-be attacker. The values of karate to modern life are numerous. In our everyday lives we often forget the value of exercise to both our physical and mental health. The practice of karate tones the body, develops coordination, quickens reflexes, and builds stamina. Also, the serious practice of karate develops composure, a clearer thought process, deeper insight into one's mental capabilities, and more self-confidence. In this, karate is not an end, but a means to an end. It is an activity in which advancing age is not a hindrance. Rather, it encourages proficiency and the keen coordination of mind and body.

events.title: Wednesday Night Bible Talk
events.description: Join us for Wednesday Night Bible Talk! We begin with an icebreaker activity, then we spend some time reading scripture together as we are led into discussion and reflection. Stick around afterwards for some fellowship!

events.title: Friday Night Devotional
events.description: Join us for Friday Night Devotional! We will start with a short devotional, then we will spend some time together in fellowship with some fun games and activities.
